context.description: German philosophical prose on perception, information, free will, and decision-making. It contrasts the "giant" and the "dwarf" as perspectives on how added information can change interpretation and action, using a reflective question about whether to continue or try an approach.
normalization_notes: Book title inferred from the source file name.
Classification normalized from raw 'book' / 'nonfiction_philosophical_writing' to the closest supported body-content theoretical-discussion page type.

rows.paragraph_text: A reflective passage argues that receiving new information does not change the rules or expand hand options, so the same decision would still be expected under the same conditions.
It explains that the added perspective can still help the reader mentally subtract displayed signals, compare appearances, and judge whether the expected value justifies continuing or trying.
